roof_age_estimate: unknown; appears established but not clearly end-of-life from aerial view
observations: Large white reflective membrane roof with generally uniform coverage.
Numerous rooftop penetrations/equipment curbs visible, but no obvious open units or missing caps from aerial view.
Some localized staining/discoloration and light soiling are visible, especially near edges and around a few penetrations.
Minor seam/patch-like linear markings are visible, but no widespread blistering, tearing, or exposed substrate is apparent.
Drainage appears generally controlled; no clear ponding areas are visible in this image resolution/lighting condition, though subtle low spots cannot be ruled out from aerial imagery alone.
summary: The roof appears to be a large low-slope single-ply membrane system in fair condition. Visible signs of wear are mostly limited to localized staining, minor surface markings, and maintenance-related discoloration around penetrations and edges, with no clear evidence of severe failure from the aerial image.
recommendation: Continue routine maintenance and targeted inspection, with emphasis on seams, perimeter flashings, and rooftop penetrations. A close-up field inspection is recommended to confirm seal integrity and to check for concealed membrane wear not visible in aerial imagery.
